title: Atomic Habits for Coders
story_hook: 1% better every day = 37x better in a year. James Clear's Atomic Habits applied to your lazy tier-4 life. This chapter makes you unstoppable.

content.story: 1% better every day = 37x better in a year. James Clear's Atomic Habits applied to your lazy tier-4 life. This chapter makes you unstoppable.

content.title: Atomic Habits For Coding Productivity
content.focus_note: Look for the 4 laws and how they map to coding routines.

content.doc_md:
The 4 laws: Make it obvious (habit stacking), attractive, easy, satisfying.

Coding environment design + 2-minute rule.

visualizer.notes: Check off each day you code - even 2 minutes counts.

practice_problems.prompt: Design your personal coding environment (desk, distractions, time).
Write your habit stack: 'after tea -> open DSA OS'.

quiz_questions.question: Which law is 'habit stacking' part of?
quiz_questions.explanation: Habit stacking makes the cue visible and obvious.

quiz_questions.question: What is the 2-minute rule?
options: Make the habit so easy you can start in 2 minutes
quiz_questions.correctAnswer: Make the habit so easy you can start in 2 minutes
quiz_questions.explanation: Starting is the hardest part, so make it tiny.

quiz_questions.question: Which law makes a habit feel rewarding?
Which law reduces friction to start?

content.task_prompt: Write your 30-day coder identity plan. Focus on the smallest daily habit you will never miss.

content.connection_map: Thinking clearly -> Reading errors -> Reading code -> Daily habits = unstoppable progress.
recall_questions: Which habit will you stack with coding?
How will you make coding easy to start?
What reward will make it satisfying?
content.identity_affirmation: You are now the kind of coder who shows up every day.
